evals.prompt: I need a simple welcome dashboard that appears in the Content section of the Umbraco backoffice. It should show a heading that says 'Welcome' and a short paragraph underneath. Nothing fancy, just the basics to get started.
evals.expected_output: A umbraco-package.json manifest and a TypeScript/JavaScript element file that together produce a working dashboard in the Content section, following current Umbraco docs patterns.
expectations: Manifest JSON has type set to 'dashboard'
Manifest JSON includes alias, name, and element fields
Manifest JSON has meta.label and meta.pathname
Manifest JSON has a condition with alias 'Umb.Condition.SectionAlias' and match 'Umb.Section.Content'
Manifest is wrapped in an extensions array inside umbraco-package.json
Element imports from '@umbraco-cms/backoffice/external/lit'
Element imports UmbLitElement from '@umbraco-cms/backoffice/lit-element'
Element class extends UmbLitElement (not UmbElementMixin(LitElement))
Element uses @customElement() decorator for registration (not customElements.define)
Element has a render() method returning html template with welcome content

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard for the Settings section that should only be visible to admin users. It should display a heading 'System Info' and a placeholder paragraph. I want it in my existing Umbraco project at App_Plugins/SystemInfo/.
evals.expected_output: A manifest with both section and admin conditions, plus an element file, both following current docs patterns.
expectations: Manifest JSON has type set to 'dashboard'
Manifest JSON has a condition with alias 'Umb.Condition.SectionAlias' and match 'Umb.Section.Settings'
Manifest JSON has a condition with alias 'Umb.Condition.CurrentUser.IsAdmin'
Manifest conditions array contains exactly 2 condition objects
Element file path references App_Plugins/SystemInfo/
Element imports UmbLitElement from '@umbraco-cms/backoffice/lit-element'
Element class extends UmbLitElement
Element uses @customElement() decorator for registration

evals.prompt: I want to add a dashboard to the Media section with a custom URL path of 'media-overview' and I'd like it to load before other dashboards. The dashboard should just show a heading for now. Put it in App_Plugins/MediaOverview/.
evals.expected_output: A manifest with weight, custom pathname, and Media section condition, plus an element file following docs patterns.
expectations: Manifest JSON has type set to 'dashboard'
Manifest JSON has meta.pathname set to 'media-overview'
Manifest JSON has meta.label field
Manifest JSON has a weight field with a numeric value
Manifest JSON has a condition with alias 'Umb.Condition.SectionAlias' and match 'Umb.Section.Media'
Element file path references App_Plugins/MediaOverview/
Element imports UmbLitElement from '@umbraco-cms/backoffice/lit-element'
Element class extends UmbLitElement
Element uses @customElement() decorator for registration
